headline: Agentic Era Arrives: Claude Code Dominates, Replit Hits $9B, Copilot Gets Claude Superpowers

stories.title: Claude Code Goes From Zero to Number One in 8 Months
stories.summary: Claude Code absolutely ate in 2026 — launched May 2025 and by early March hit 46% "most loved" rating among devs. Cursor's at 19%, Copilot's sitting at 9%, and basically everyone's using 2-3 tools now depending on the task.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code's agentic workflow (no tab completions, just prompts → review) flipped the entire landscape. It's lowkey the best for big refactors and multi-file changes, but the pricing tiers ($20 → $100 → $200) can sting.

stories.title: Replit Closes $400M at $9B Valuation — 3x Jump
stories.summary: Replit literally tripled its valuation after previous round. The vibe coding OG now has a full cloud IDE with Ghostwriter, Agent, and hosting built-in. Databricks, Okta, Shaq, YC all in.
stories.takeaway: Replit's not just generating code anymore — it's the full stack (database, auth, deployment). Built-in security scanner catches AI mistakes before deploy. This is why founders are shipping MVPs in weeks instead of months.

stories.title: Microsoft Adds Claude to Copilot (March 9) — The Plot Thickens
stories.summary: Microsoft literally buried the lede: enterprise users can now pick Claude over GPT in M365, Copilot Studio, and GitHub Copilot. Wave 3 went full multi-model. Anthropic became a Microsoft subprocessor on Jan 6.
stories.takeaway: This is wild fr — Microsoft invested billions in Anthropic while having a $135B stake in OpenAI. They essentially said "our partner's model isn't good enough for everything," which is the most honest product review ever. Now Copilot's not locked into one vendor.

stories.title: Simon Willison on Agentic Engineering: "The New Thing Is You Don't Read the Code"
stories.summary: At Pragmatic Summit (March 14), Simon dropped a fireside chat breakdown on how devs are adopting AI coding in phases. Started with ChatGPT questions, then agents writing bits of code, then agents writing MORE code than humans. Now? "Three weeks ago, you don't read the code."
stories.takeaway: The lethal trifecta warning is real — sandboxing agents is critical. Devs are legit trusting AI without review cycles now, which is lowkey terrifying if you're thinking security. But also... that's the efficiency frontier.

watchNext.title: The Real Cost of AI Coding in 2026 (Token Burn & Hidden Fees)
watchNext.why: Every tool says "build free" but token costs are wild. Taskade Genesis at $6/mo vs Lovable at $25+ vs Bolt's per-request model. Need to know what you're actually paying for before scaling.

watchNext.title: GitHub Copilot Student Plan Restrictions (March 12 Update)
watchNext.why: Two million students just got their model selection taken away. Can't manually pick Claude Opus or GPT-5.4 anymore — stuck with Auto mode. Wild move mid-semester.
